articles.topic: Strength training reduces women's heart attack risk
articles.headline: Two Hours of Weights a Week Cuts Women's Heart Attack Risk by Nearly Half
articles.body: A massive study of over 117,000 women just rewrote the playbook on preventing heart disease. Researchers found that women who do at least two hours of strength training per week have a 44% lower risk of heart attack compared to women who don't lift. Even better: when combined with 150 minutes of aerobic exercise weekly, the benefit jumps to 45% lower risk. This isn't marginal. Statins reduce heart attack risk by 25-33%. Strength training is doing the heavy lifting your heart desperately needs. The cardiovascular benefits held up even after adjusting for BMI, diabetes, and high blood pressure.
articles.takeaway: Two hours of weekly strength training is a more powerful heart protection tool than most women realize—rival any medication.
articles.source: Medical News Today / Journal of the American College of Cardiology

articles.topic: Minimally invasive procedure replaces knee replacement
articles.headline: Skip Knee Surgery. This 15-Minute Procedure Delivers the Same Relief.
articles.body: Genicular artery embolization—GAE—sounds complex. It's actually elegant: a catheter blocks abnormal blood vessels fueling knee inflammation, pain signals stop, and you're home the same day. No cutting bone. No anesthesia. Most people resume normal activities within 48-72 hours. A year later, nearly all report pain relief equal to total knee replacement. About 70% of patients cut their pain scores in half or more. The procedure works best for mild-to-moderate osteoarthritis, and pain relief lasts two to four years. For anyone who's failed injections and isn't ready for surgery, GAE fills a real gap.
articles.takeaway: If your knees are screaming but you're not ready for surgery, ask your doctor about GAE before booking a replacement.
articles.source: ScienceDaily / University of Colorado Anschutz School of Medicine

articles.topic: Hidden gut bacterium linked to longevity
articles.headline: Scientists Found a Mystery Bacteria That Shows Up in Healthy People Worldwide
articles.body: A global study uncovered a hidden bacterium—CAG-170—that appears consistently in healthy people but is nearly absent in those with chronic disease. This microbe helps your gut digest food and maintains a robust microbial ecosystem. The discovery could redefine what a healthy microbiome actually looks like and reshape how we think about probiotics. Researchers say CAG-170 might become a marker of true gut health, similar to how grip strength predicts longevity. Right now, we measure gut health too broadly. This finding suggests we should start looking at specific bacterial players that separate the thriving from the struggling.
articles.takeaway: Your microbiome isn't just about diversity—specific bacteria like CAG-170 appear to separate healthy aging from disease.

articles.topic: Amino acid methionine improves immune survival
articles.headline: One Simple Amino Acid Dramatically Boosted Survival in Severe Infection
articles.body: A Salk Institute study found something surprising: methionine, a dietary amino acid, dramatically improved survival in mice facing severe infections and inflammatory conditions. The mechanism doesn't involve forcing the immune system into overdrive—instead, methionine helped kidneys function better, which indirectly strengthened immune resilience. Methionine is already in protein-rich foods: chicken, beef, eggs, cheese. You don't need a supplement to get it. But the finding points to how foundational amino acids support recovery from systemic stress. Most people think immunity means attacking harder. This research suggests it also means supporting the organs that manage the aftermath.
articles.takeaway: Don't overlook basic amino acid sufficiency. Adequate protein intake supports the deep systems that actually win fights against infection.

quote.text: The resistance against Negritude wasn't just about the Negritude poets—it was about me. That's the role that makes it valid, that gives it life.
quote.author: Audre Lorde
quote.context: Poet, essayist, and theorist on power, identity, and the courage required to live authentically

workout.title: Sun Salutation Flow: Breath & Movement Foundation
workout.tip: Synchronize each movement with a single breath—inhale for the upward flow, exhale for the forward fold. This rhythm activates your parasympathetic nervous system and primes your body for recovery.
workout.warmup: 3 min: 5 gentle neck rolls each direction, 10 shoulder rolls backward, 5 cat-cow flows, 5 slow spinal twists seated

exercises.name: Sun Salutation A (Surya Namaskar A)
exercises.notes: Establish a 1-breath-per-movement tempo: inhale mountain pose, exhale fold forward, inhale halfway lift, exhale plank, inhale up-dog, exhale down-dog. Hold down-dog 3 breaths between rounds. Focus on spinal extension and shoulder mobility.

exercises.name: Mountain Pose Hold (Tadasana)
exercises.notes: Press all four corners of your feet evenly into the mat. Engage your thighs, lengthen your spine, and breathe deeply. This resets posture between flow sets and builds awareness of neutral alignment.

exercises.name: Forward Fold with Gentle Hang (Uttanasana)
exercises.notes: Soften your knees, let gravity work, and avoid forcing depth. Let your head hang heavy to release neck tension. This opens the posterior chain and reduces lower-back tightness from sitting.

workout.cooldown: 5 min: child's pose 1 min, supine figure-4 stretch 1 min each side, reclined twist 1 min each side, final savasana 1 min with deep belly breathing
workout.description: A gentle, rhythmic introduction to sun salutations for beginners seeking body awareness and flowing breath work. This 30-minute session builds heat gradually, improves flexibility, and teaches the foundational link between breath and movement—essential for any athlete looking to recover actively and reduce injury risk.

meal.title: Beef Jerky & Sharp Cheddar Snack Pack
meal.description: Portable, shelf-stable protein and fat combo designed to fuel strength athletes between workouts or as a quick desk snack. The combination of beef jerky's concentrated protein and aged cheddar's calcium supports muscle maintenance and bone density.
meal.instructions:
1. Open jerky package.
2. Portion cheddar into a small container or wrap in parchment.
3. Pack together for a grab-and-go snack.
meal.tip: Buy jerky in bulk from Costco or online to cut costs. Choose low-sugar varieties (under 4g per serving) to avoid unnecessary carbs between meals.
